showWorkloadSummary(summary) {\n890\t            const el = document.getElementById('workloadSummary');\n891\t            const parts = [];\n892\t\n893\t            if (summary.pending_intents > 0) {\n894\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n895\t            }\n896\t            if (summary.new_results > 0) {\n897\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.new_results}</strong> new results`);\n898\t            }\n899\t            if (summary.unresolved_exceptions > 0) {\n900\t                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.unresolved_exceptions}</strong> unresolved exceptions`);\n901\t            }\n902\t\n903\t            if (parts.length > 0) {\n904\t                el.innerHTML = '📊   }\n910\t\n911\t        async function registerSurface() {\n912\t            try {\n913\t                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n914\t                    method: }\n985\t\n986\t        function connectSSE() {\n987\t            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n988\t\n989\t            const url = <div class=\"container\">\n        <header>\n            <div style=\"display:flex;align-items:baseline;gap:8px\">\n                <h1>ADC Canvas</h1>\n                <span id=\"versionBadge\" style=\"font-size:12px;color:#475569;font-weight:500;font-family:'Monaco','Menlo',monospace\"></span>\n            </div>\n            <div class=\"status\">\n                <span class=\"status-dot\" id=\"statusDot\"></span>\n                <span id=\"statusText\">Connecting...</span>\n            </div>\n        </header>\n\n        <div class=\"workload-summary\" id=\"workloadSummary\"></div>\n\n        <div class=\"input-bar\">\n            <textarea\n                id=\"inputTextarea\"\n                class=\"input-textarea\"\n                placeholder=\"Ask anything — status of the options pipeline, what's in flight, open beads…\"\n                rows=\"1\"\n            ></textarea>\n            <button class=\"btn-mic\" id=\"btnMic\" title=\"Voice input\">\n                <svg width=\"20\" height=\"20\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">\n                    <path d=\"M12 2a3 3 0 0 0-3 3v7a3 3 0 0 0 6 0V5a3 3 0 0 0-3-3z\"/>\n                    <path d=\"M19 10v2a7 7 0 0 1-14 0v-2\"/>\n                    <line x1=\"12\" y1=\"19\" x2=\"12\" y2=\"23\"/>\n                    <line x1=\"8\" y1=\"23\" x2=\"16\" y2=\"23\"/>\n                </svg>\n            </button>\n            <button class=\"btn-send\" id=\"btnSend\" title=\"Send\" disabled>\n                <svg width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">\n                    <line x1=\"22\" y1=\"2\" x2=\"11\" y2=\"13\"/>\n                    <polygon points=\"22 2 15 22 11 13 2 9 22 2\"/>\n                </svg>\n            </button>\n        </div>\n\n        <div id=\"topicsContainer\" class=\"topics-grid\">\n            <div class=\"loading\">\n                <div class=\"spinner\"></div>\n                <span>Loading topics...</span>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <!-- Render helpers (createTopicCard + friends) — loaded before the app\n         script so they're available as globals and unit-testable headlessly. -->\n    <script src=\"/canvas.js\"></script>\n    <script>\n        // Session and surface IDs from URL params or generate new\n        const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);\n        const sessionId = urlParams.get('session_id') || generateId();\n        const surfaceType = 'canvas';\n\n        let surfaceId = null;\n        let eventSource = null;\n\n        // Track pending placeholders so we can find/replace them later.\n        // Key: pendingId (utterance_id), Value: placeholder HTMLElement.\n        var pendingPlaceholders = {};\n\n        // Update URL with session_id for reload persistence\n        if (!urlParams.get('session_id')) {\n            urlParams.set('session_id', sessionId);\n            window.history.replaceState({}, '', `${window.location.pathname}?${urlParams}`);\n        }\n\n        function generateId() {\n            return 'adc-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 16);\n        }\n\n        // createTopicCard / escapeHtml / formatStaleness / getStalenessLevel live\n        // in /canvas.js (loaded below as a blocking <script src> before this\n        // block), so they are available as globals here and unit-testable\n        // headlessly. See src/canvas/canvas.js.\n\n        function updateConnectionStatus(status, text) {\n            const dot = document.getElementById('statusDot');\n            const statusText = document.getElementById('statusText');\n\n            dot.className = 'status-dot ' + status;\n            statusText.textContent = text;\n        }\n\n        function showWorkloadSummary(summary) {\n            const el = document.getElementById('workloadSummary');\n            const parts = [];\n\n            if (summary.pending_intents > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n            }\n            if (summary.new_results > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.new_results}</strong> new results`);\n            }\n            if (summary.unresolved_exceptions > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.unresolved_exceptions}</strong> unresolved exceptions`);\n            }\n\n            if (parts.length > 0) {\n                el.innerHTML = '📊 While you were away: ' + parts.join(', ');\n                el.classList.add('show');\n            } else {\n                el.classList.remove('show');\n            }\n        }\n\n        async function registerSurface() {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        session_id: sessionId,\n                        surface_type: surfaceType\n                    })\n                });\n\n                if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to register surface');\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                surfaceId = data.surface_id;\n                return surfaceId;\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error registering surface:', error);\n                throw error;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Registry project list for the first-run welcome card\n        // (config/registry.yaml → GET /api/v1/registry). DB/result independent —\n        // the welcome card renders even against an empty component store. Cached\n        // after the first fetch so the per-SSE-event reloads don't refetch it.\n        let welcomeProjects = null;\n\n        async function loadWelcomeProjects() {\n            if (welcomeProjects) return welcomeProjects;\n            try {\n                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/registry');\n                if (resp.ok) {\n                    const data = await resp.json();\n                    welcomeProjects = (data && data.projects) || [];\n                    return welcomeProjects;\n                }\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading registry for welcome card:', error);\n            }\n            // Graceful fallback: an empty list still renders the welcome card\n            // (with default example utterances), so a wedged registry endpoint\n            // never leaves the canvas bare.\n            welcomeProjects = [];\n            return welcomeProjects;\n        }\n\n        async function loadTopics() {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/topics`);\n                if (!response.ok) return;\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n                const cards = data.cards || [];\n\n                // Fresh session (zero topics): show the first-run welcome card\n                // instead of the bare empty-state. Its project list + example\n                // utterances come from the registry (never the DB), so we only\n                // need to fetch it on the zero-card branch. The welcome card is\n                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n                const projects = cards.length ? [] : await loadWelcomeProjects();\n\n                container.innerHTML = '';\n                buildContainerChildren(cards, projects).forEach(node => {\n                    container.appendChild(node);\n                });\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading topics:', error);\n            }\n        }\n\n        function connectSSE() {\n            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n\n            const url = `/api/v1/sse?surface_id=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceId)}&session_id=${encodeURIComponent(sessionId)}&surface_type=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceType)}`;\n            eventSource = new EventSource(url);\n\n            eventSource.onopen = () => {\n                updateConnectionStatus('', 'Connected');\n                loadTopics();\n            };\n\n            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n            };\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('connected', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('SSE connected:', data);\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('workload_summary', (event) => {\n                const summary = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                showWorkloadSummary(summary);\n            });\n\n            // Dispatch ack: backup path for intent_ids if the fetch response didn't\n            // include them (e.g., the response arrived before the intent was routed).\n            eventSource.addEventListener('dispatch_ack', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const utteranceId = data.utterance_id;\n                const intentIds = data.intent_ids;\n                const utterance = data.utterance;\n\n                if (!utteranceId || !intentIds || !Array.isArray(intentIds)) return;\n\n                // Check if there's an existing placeholder to split\n                const placeholder = pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n                const createdAt = placeholder\n                    ? parseInt(placeholder.dataset.createdAt, 10)\n                    : Date.now();\n\n                // Split into per-thread pending cards\n                const threadCards = splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds);\n\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n                    // Replace the placeholder with the thread cards\n                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n                    placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n                } else {\n                    // No placeholder (test scenario or race condition): prepend thread cards\n                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n                    container.insertBefore(frag, container.firstChild);\n                }\n\n                // No longer track this placeholder\n                delete pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n            });\n\n            // Thread-level progress event\n            eventSource.addEventListener('thread_progress', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const threadId = data.thread_id;\n\n                if (!threadId) {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: missing thread_id in event data');\n                    return;\n                }\n\n                // Find the pending thread card by thread_id\n                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + threadId + '\"]');\n                if (!threadCard) {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard not found for thread_id=' + threadId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (!threadCard.classList.contains('pending-card')) {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard does not have pending-card class for thread_id=' + threadId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: _setProgress is not a function');\n                    return;\n                }\n\n                // Pass progress message (e.g., '3/5 sources in') to _setProgress()\n                const completed = data.completed;\n                const total = data.total;\n                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n            });\n\n            // Per-source progress update for a thread\n            eventSource.addEventListener('progress_update', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                const completed = data.completed;\n                const total = data.total;\n\n                if (!intentId) return;\n\n                // Find the pending thread card by intent_id\n                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                if (!threadCard) {\n                    console.warn('progress_update: threadCard is null for intent_id=' + intentId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (!threadCard.classList.contains('pending-card')) {\n                    console.warn('progress_update: threadCard does not have pending-card class for intent_id=' + intentId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n                    console.warn('progress_update: _setProgress is not a function');\n                    return;\n                }\n                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('result_created', (event) => {\n                const result = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('New result:', result);\n\n                // Find and remove the pending card for this intent before reloading\n                const intentId = result.intent_id;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        // Remove the pending card so it doesn't flicker when loadTopics() renders the real result\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for intent_id:', intentId);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                loadTopics(); // Reload topics to show new result\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('topic_updated', (event) => {\n                const topic = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Topic updated:', topic);\n                loadTopics();\n            });\n\n            // Task stuck event: render stuck card when a bead is fenced\n            eventSource.addEventListener('task_stuck', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Task stuck:', data);\n\n                // Find and remove any pending card for this intent\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for stuck intent_id:', intentId);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Create and prepend the stuck card\n                const stuckCard = createStuckCard(data);\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                // Clear loading/empty state if present\n                if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                    container.innerHTML = '';\n                }\n\n                // Prepend stuck card (newest first)\n                container.insertBefore(stuckCard, container.firstChild);\n            });\n\n            // Task failed event: render failed card on terminal failure\n            eventSource.addEventListener('task_failed', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Task failed:', data);\n\n                // Find and remove any pending card for this intent\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for failed intent_id:', intentId);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Create and prepend the failed card\n                const failedCard = createFailedCard(data);\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                // Clear loading/empty state if present\n                if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                    container.innerHTML = '';\n                }\n\n                // Prepend failed card (newest first)\n                container.insertBefore(failedCard, container.firstChild);\n            });\n\n            // Error event: render error card from degraded-state SSE error events\n            eventSource.addEventListener('error', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Error event:', data);\n\n                // Find and remove any pending card for this intent/pending before rendering error\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                const pendingId = data.pending_id;\n                let selector = null;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    selector = '[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]';\n                } else if (pendingId) {\n                    selector = '[data-pending-id=\"' + pendingId + '\"]';\n                }\n\n                if (selector) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector(selector);\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for error event:', selector);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Create and prepend the error card\n                const errorCard = createErrorCard(data);\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                // Clear loading/empty state if present\n                if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                    container.innerHTML = '';\n                }\n\n                // Prepend error card (newest first)\n                container.insertBefore(errorCard, container.firstChild);\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('disconnect', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Server disconnect:', data);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n                eventSource.close();\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Elapsed-time ticker for pending cards\n        // Every second, update the elapsed-time footer on all pending cards\n        // and apply aged treatment if they've exceeded the 30s threshold.\n        setInterval(function () {\n            var now = Date.now();\n            var pendingCards = document.querySelectorAll('.pending-card[data-pending-id]');\n            pendingCards.forEach(function (card) {\n                tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n                applyAgedTreatment(card, now);\n            });\n        }, 1000);\n\n        // Heartbeat to keep surface alive\n        setInterval(() => {\n            if (surfaceId) {\n                fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        surface_id: surfaceId,\n                        session_id: sessionId\n                    })\n                }).catch(console.error);\n            }\n        }, 30000); // Every 30 seconds\n\n        // ── Input bar ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n        const textarea = document.getElementById('inputTextarea');\n        const btnSend  = document.getElementById('btnSend');\n        const btnMic   = document.getElementById('btnMic');\n\n        // Auto-resize textarea\n        textarea.addEventListener('input', () => {\n            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n            textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n            btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n        });\n\n        // Send on Enter (Shift+Enter = newline)\n        textarea.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n            if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {\n                e.preventDefault();\n                if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n            }\n        });\n\n        btnSend.addEventListener('click', () => {\n            if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n        });\n\n        async function dispatch(utterance) {\n            if (!utterance) return;\n            textarea.value = '';\n            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n            btnSend.disabled = true;\n\n            // Generate a client-side pending ID for the placeholder. We'll swap it\n            // for the server's utterance_id when the response arrives.\n            var pendingId = 'pending-' + Date.now() + '-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 8);\n            var createdAt = Date.now();\n\n            // Create the pending placeholder NOW, before the server round-trip,\n            // so a hung server still leaves a card on canvas to age.\n            var placeholder = createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId);\n            var container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n            // Clear the empty-state/loading The built-in builders below all go through the\n22\t * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n23\t * escaping contract.\n24\t *\n25\t * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n26\t * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n27\t * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n28\t * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n29\t */\n30\t\n31\t// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n32\t// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n33\t// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\n34\tif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n35\t    var document = global.document;\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n39\t// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n40\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n41\t\n42\t// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n43\t// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n44\t// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n45\t// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\n46\tvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n47\t\n48\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n49\t// Existing topic-card render helpers\n50\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n51\t\n52\tfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n53\t    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n54\t    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n55\t    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n56\t    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\tfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n60\t    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n61\t    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n62\t    return 'very-stale';\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\t/**\n66\t * Build a topic card from topic data.\n67\t *\n68\t * @param {TopicCardData} cardData - Topic card data structure\n69\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .topic-card element (or fallback-card element if card_fallback is set)\n70\t */\n71\tfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n72\t    const topic = cardData.topic;\n73\t    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n74\t    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n75\t\n76\t    // When the result has card_fallback=True, render the built-in generic fallback card\n77\t    // instead of the standard topic card. The fallback card is a complete card showing\n78\t    // result.data as a key/value grid (plan: Component Library → \"Built-in generic\n79\t    // fallback card\"). Topic metadata is preserved via data attributes for staleness\n80\t    // tracking and querying, but visually the card is the fallback presentation.\n81\t    if (latestResult && latestResult.card_fallback) {\n82\t        const fallbackCard = createFallbackCard(latestResult);\n83\t        // Preserve topic metadata for staleness tracking and querying\n84\t        fallbackCard.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n85\t        fallbackCard.dataset.topicType = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n86\t        fallbackCard.dataset.cardFallback = '1';\n87\t        return fallbackCard;\n88\t    }\n89\t\n90\t    // When the result has rendered_html (from hot-path component match), inject it directly.\n91\t    // The HTML is already escaped server-side (hot-path renderer's escaping contract),\n92\t    // so we inject it as-is. Topic metadata is preserved via data attributes.\n93\t    if (latestResult && latestResult.rendered_html) {\n94\t        const card = document.createElement('div');\n95\t        card.className = 'topic-card component-card';\n96\t        card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n97\t        card.dataset.componentId = latestResult.component_id || '';\n98\t\n99\t        const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n100\t        card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n101\t\n102\t        const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n103\t        card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n104\t\n105\t        // Build the full card HTML: component content + staleness indicator\n106\t        const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n107\t        const html = latestResult.rendered_html + `\n108\t            <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n109\t                <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n110\t                <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n111\t            </div>`;\n112\t\n113\t        card.innerHTML = html;\n114\t        return card;\n115\t    }\n116\t\n117\t    const card = document.createElement('div');\n118\t    card.className = 'topic-card';\n119\t    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n120\t\n121\t    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n122\t    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n123\t\n124\t    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n125\t\n126\t    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n127\t    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n128\t    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n129\t    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n130\t    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n131\t\n132\t    let html = `\n133\t        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n134\t            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n135\t            <div>\n136\t                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${escapeHtml(topic.type || 'adhoc')}</span>\n137\t                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n138\t            </div>\n139\t        </div>\n140\t    `;\n141\t\n142\t    // Add latest result if available\n143\t    if (latestResult) {\n144\t        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n145\t        html += `\n146\t            <div class=\"result-content\">\n147\t                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n148\t                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n149\t                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${escapeHtml(urgencyClass)}</span>\n150\t                </div>\n151\t        `;\n152\t\n153\t        if (latestResult.data) {\n154\t            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n155\t        }\n156\t\n157\t        html += `</div>`;\n158\t    }\n159\t\n160\t    // Add staleness indicator\n161\t    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n162\t    html += `\n163\t        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n164\t            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n165\t            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n166\t        </div>\n167\t    `;\n168\t\n169\t    card.innerHTML = html;\n170\t    return card;\n171\t}\n172\t\n173\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n174\t// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n175\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n176\t\n177\tfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n178\t    const div = document.createElement('div');\n179\t    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n180\t    return div.innerHTML;\n181\t}\n182\t\n183\t/**\n184\t * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n185\t *\n186\t * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n187\t * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n188\t * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n189\t * inserted verbatim as text.\n190\t */\n191\tfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n192\t    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n193\t    if (className) node.className = className;\n194\t    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n195\t        if (c == null) return;\n196\t        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n197\t            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n198\t        } else {\n199\t            node.appendChild(c);\n200\t        }\n201\t    });\n202\t    return node;\n203\t}\n204\t\n205\t// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\n206\tfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n207\t    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n208\t    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n209\t    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n210\t    const rem = s % 60;\n211\t    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n212\t}\n213\t\n214\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n215\t// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n216\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n217\t\n218\t// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n219\t// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\n220\tvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n221\t    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n222\t    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n223\t    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n224\t];\n225\t\n226\t// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n227\t// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n228\t// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n229\t// projects' supported intents\").\n230\tvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n231\t    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n232\t    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n233\t    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n234\t    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n235\t    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n236\t    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n237\t    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n238\t    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n239\t};\n240\t\n241\tfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n242\t    const out = [];\n243\t    const seen = {};\n244\t    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n245\t    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n246\t    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n247\t    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n248\t        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n249\t        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n250\t        return ad - bd;\n251\t    });\n252\t    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n253\t        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n254\t        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n255\t            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n256\t            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n257\t            if (!tmpl) return;\n258\t            seen[intent] = true;\n259\t            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n260\t        });\n261\t    });\n262\t    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n263\t}\n264\t\n265\t// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n266\t// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\n267\tvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n268\t\n269\t/**\n270\t * Build the first-run welcome card.\n271\t *\n272\t * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n273\t * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n274\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n275\t *\n276\t * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n277\t * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n278\t * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n279\t */\n280\tfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n281\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n282\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n283\t\n284\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n285\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n286\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n287\t    ]));\n288\t\n289\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n290\t        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n291\t    ]));\n292\t\n293\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n294\t    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n295\t    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n296\t        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n297\t        const desc = p.description || '';\n298\t        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n299\t        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n300\t        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n301\t        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n302\t        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n303\t        list.appendChild(li);\n304\t    });\n305\t    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n306\t        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n307\t    }\n308\t    card.appendChild(list);\n309\t\n310\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n311\t    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n312\t    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n313\t        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n314\t    });\n315\t    card.appendChild(examples);\n316\t\n317\t    return card;\n318\t}\n319\t\n320\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n321\t// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n322\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n323\t\n324\t/**\n325\t * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n326\t *\n327\t * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n328\t * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n329\t * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n330\t * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n331\t * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n332\t * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n333\t *\n334\t * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n335\t * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n336\t * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n337\t * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n338\t *\n339\t * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n340\t * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n341\t * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n342\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n343\t */\n344\tfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n345\t    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n346\t        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n347\t    }\n348\t    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n349\t}\n350\t\n351\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n352\t// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n353\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n354\t\n355\t/**\n356\t * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n357\t *\n358\t * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n359\t * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n360\t * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n361\t * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n362\t *\n363\t * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n364\t * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n365\t * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n366\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n367\t */\n368\tfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n369\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n370\t    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n371\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n372\t    return card;\n373\t}\n374\t\n375\t/**\n376\t * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n377\t * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n378\t *\n379\t * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n380\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n381\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n382\t * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n383\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n384\t */\n385\tfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n386\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n387\t    card.classList.add('thread');\n388\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n389\t    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n390\t    return card;\n391\t}\n392\t\n393\t/**\n394\t * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n395\t * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n396\t * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n397\t * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n398\t * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n399\t * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n400\t * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n401\t *\n402\t * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n403\t * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n404\t * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n405\t * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n406\t *\n407\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n408\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n409\t * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n410\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n411\t */\n412\tfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n413\t    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n414\t        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n415\t    });\n416\t}\n417\t\n418\t// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\n419\tfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n420\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n421\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n422\t    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n423\t    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n424\t\n425\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n426\t        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n427\t        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n428\t    ]));\n429\t\n430\t    if (utterance) {\n431\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n432\t    }\n433\t\n434\t    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n435\t    progress.style.display = 'none';\n436\t    card.appendChild(progress);\n437\t\n438\t    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n439\t        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n440\t    ]);\n441\t    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n442\t\n443\t    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n444\t    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n445\t    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n446\t    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n447\t    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n448\t    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n449\t    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n450\t\n451\t    return card;\n452\t}\n453\t\n454\tfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n455\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n456\t    if (!node || !progress) return;\n457\t    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n458\t    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n459\t    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n460\t    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n461\t    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n462\t    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n463\t}\n464\t\n465\t/**\n466\t * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n467\t * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n468\t * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n469\t */\n470\tfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n471\t    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n472\t    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n473\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n474\t    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n475\t    return elapsedMs;\n476\t}\n477\t\n478\t/**\n479\t * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n480\t * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n481\t * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n482\t */\n483\tfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n484\t    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n485\t    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n486\t    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n487\t    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n488\t    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n489\t    return aged;\n490\t}\n491\t\n492\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n493\t// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n494\t//     from SSE error events\n495\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n496\t\n497\t// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n498\t// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n499\t// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\n500\tvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n501\t    router_unavailable: {\n502\t        title: 'Router unavailable',\n503\t        icon: '🔌',\n504\t        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n505\t    },\n506\t    all_sources_failed: {\n507\t        title: 'No data available',\n508\t        icon: '📭',\n509\t        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n510\t    },\n511\t    synthesis_failed: {\n512\t        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n513\t        icon: '⚠️',\n514\t        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n515\t    },\n516\t    malformed_router_output: {\n517\t        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n518\t        icon: '🧩',\n519\t        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n520\t    },\n521\t    no_match: {\n522\t        title: 'No matching project',\n523\t        icon: '❓',\n524\t        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n525\t    }\n526\t};\n527\t\n528\tfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n529\t    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n530\t    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n531\t    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n532\t}\n533\t\n534\t/**\n535\t * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n536\t *\n537\t * @param {ErrorCardData} data - Error card data structure\n538\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n539\t *\n540\t * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n541\t * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n542\t */\n543\tfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n544\t    data = data || {};\n545\t    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n546\t    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n547\t\n548\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n549\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n550\t    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n551\t    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n552\t    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n553\t\n554\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n555\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n556\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n557\t    ]));\n558\t\n559\t    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n560\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n561\t\n562\t    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n563\t    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n564\t    if (data.utterance) {\n565\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n566\t            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n567\t            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n568\t        ]));\n569\t    }\n570\t\n571\t    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n572\t    const sources = data.sources;\n573\t    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n574\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n575\t        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n576\t            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n577\t            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n578\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n579\t                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n580\t                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n581\t            ]));\n582\t        });\n583\t        card.appendChild(ul);\n584\t    }\n585\t\n586\t    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n587\t    if (data.data != null) {\n588\t        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n589\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n590\t        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n591\t    }\n592\t\n593\t    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n594\t    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n595\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n596\t        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n597\t            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n598\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n599\t        });\n600\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n601\t            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n602\t            ul\n603\t        ]));\n604\t    }\n605\t\n606\t    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n607\t    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n608\t        ? 'Edit & resend'\n609\t        : 'Retry';\n610\t    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n611\t\n612\t    return card;\n613\t}\n614\t\n615\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n616\t// Canvas Card Type Definitions\n617\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n618\t\n619\t/**\n620\t * @typedef {Object} StuckCardData\n621\t * @property {string} bead_id - } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n848\t    }\n849\t    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n850\t        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n851\t    }\n852\t    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n853\t        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n854\t    }\n855\t    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n856\t}\n857\t\n858\tfunction _stringify(v) {\n859\t    if (v == null) return '';\n860\t    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n861\t    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n862\t    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n863\t}\n864\t\n865\t// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n866\t// function declarations).
