* ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n *\n * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n * topic-card element, and that build the four BUILT-IN card families the plan\n * ships as fixed templates in this served frontend (Component Library →\n * \"Built-in cards\"): (1) generic fallback, (2) first-run welcome card, (3)\n * pending/ack cards (submit-time placeholder, per-thread pending with\n * per-source progress, aged-pending treatment), and (4) the error/clarification\n * family from Degraded-State UX. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n *\n * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n * escaping contract.\n *\n * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n */\n\n// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\nif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n    var document = global.document;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\nvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Existing topic-card render helpers\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n}\n\nfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n    return 'very-stale';\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a topic card from topic data.\n *\n * @param {TopicCardData} cardData - Topic card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .topic-card element (or fallback-card element if card_fallback is set)\n */\nfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n    const topic = cardData.topic;\n    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n\n    // When the result has card_fallback=True, render the built-in generic fallback card\n    // instead of the standard topic card. The fallback card is a complete card showing\n    // result.data as a key/value grid (plan: Component Library → \"Built-in generic\n    // fallback card\"). Topic metadata is preserved via data attributes for staleness\n    // tracking and querying, but visually the card is the fallback presentation.\n    if (latestResult && latestResult.card_fallback) {\n        const fallbackCard = createFallbackCard(latestResult);\n        // Preserve topic metadata for staleness tracking and querying\n        fallbackCard.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n        fallbackCard.dataset.topicType = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n        fallbackCard.dataset.cardFallback = '1';\n        return fallbackCard;\n    }\n\n    // When the result has rendered_html (from hot-path component match), inject it directly.\n    // The HTML is already escaped server-side (hot-path renderer's escaping contract),\n    // so we inject it as-is. Topic metadata is preserved via data attributes.\n    if (latestResult && latestResult.rendered_html) {\n        const card = document.createElement('div');\n        card.className = 'topic-card component-card';\n        card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n        card.dataset.componentId = latestResult.component_id || '';\n\n        const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n        card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n\n        const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n        card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n\n        // Build the full card HTML: component content + staleness indicator\n        const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n        const html = latestResult.rendered_html + `\n            <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n                <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n                <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n            </div>`;\n\n        card.innerHTML = html;\n        return card;\n    }\n\n    const card = document.createElement('div');\n    card.className = 'topic-card';\n    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n\n    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n\n    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n\n    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n\n    let html = `\n        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n            <div>\n                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${escapeHtml(topic.type || 'adhoc')}</span>\n                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    // Add latest result if available\n    if (latestResult) {\n        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n        html += `\n            <div class=\"result-content\">\n                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${escapeHtml(urgencyClass)}</span>\n                </div>\n        `;\n\n        if (latestResult.data) {\n            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n        }\n\n        html += `</div>`;\n    }\n\n    // Add staleness indicator\n    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n    html += `\n        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n        </div>\n    `;\n\n    card.innerHTML = html;\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n    const div = document.createElement('div');\n    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n    return div.innerHTML;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n *\n * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n * inserted verbatim as text.\n */\nfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n    if (className) node.className = className;\n    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n        if (c == null) return;\n        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n        } else {\n            node.appendChild(c);\n        }\n    });\n    return node;\n}\n\n// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\nfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n    const rem = s % 60;\n    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\nvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n];\n\n// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n// projects' supported intents\").\nvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n};\n\nfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n    const out = [];\n    const seen = {};\n    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n        return ad - bd;\n    });\n    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n            if (!tmpl) return;\n            seen[intent] = true;\n            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n        });\n    });\n    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n}\n\n// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\nvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n\n/**\n * Build the first-run welcome card.\n *\n * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n *\n * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n */\nfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n    ]));\n\n    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n    ]));\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n        const desc = p.description || '';\n        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n        list.appendChild(li);\n    });\n    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n    }\n    card.appendChild(list);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n    });\n    card.appendChild(examples);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n *\n * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n *\n * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n *\n * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n */\nfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n    }\n    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n *\n * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n *\n * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n */\nfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n    card.classList.add('thread');\n    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n *\n * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n *\n * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n */\nfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n    });\n}\n\n// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\nfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n    }\n\n    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n    progress.style.display = 'none';\n    card.appendChild(progress);\n\n    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n    ]);\n    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n\n    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\nfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n    if (!node || !progress) return;\n    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n}\n\n/**\n * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n */\nfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n    return elapsedMs;\n}\n\n/**\n * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n */\nfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n    return aged;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n//     from SSE error events\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\nvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n    router_unavailable: {\n        title: 'Router unavailable',\n        icon: '🔌',\n        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n    },\n    all_sources_failed: {\n        title: 'No data available',\n        icon: '📭',\n        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n    },\n    synthesis_failed: {\n        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n        icon: '⚠️',\n        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n    },\n    malformed_router_output: {\n        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n        icon: '🧩',\n        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n    },\n    no_match: {\n        title: 'No matching project',\n        icon: '❓',\n        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n    }\n};\n\nfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n}\n\n/**\n * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n *\n * @param {ErrorCardData} data - Error card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n *\n * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n */\nfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n    ]));\n\n    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n\n    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n    if (data.utterance) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n    const sources = data.sources;\n    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n            ]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(ul);\n    }\n\n    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n    if (data.data != null) {\n        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n    }\n\n    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n            ul\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n        ? 'Edit & resend'\n        : 'Retry';\n    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Canvas Card Type Definitions\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} StuckCardData\n * @property {string} bead_id - The bead reference ID that is stuck\n * @property {string} stuck_reason - The reason why the bead is stuck (refusal reason)\n * @property {number} [refusal_count] - Number of refusals that caused the stuck state\n * @property {string} [message] - Optional message describing the stuck state\n * @property {string} [action_hint] - Optional hint for user action to resolve\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} FailedCardData\n * @property {string} bead_id - The bead reference ID that failed\n * @property {string} failure_reason - The reason why the task failed\n * @property {string} [error_type] - Optional error type/category\n * @property {string} [message] - Optional message describing the failure\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} ErrorCardData\n * @property {string} error_type - The error variant type\n * @property {string} [utterance] - The original user utterance\n * @property {string} [detail] - Error detail message\n * @property {Array<{name?: string, source?: string, reason?: string, error?: string}>} [sources] - Failed sources list\n * @property {*} [data] - Raw degraded data\n * @property {string} [intent_id] - Associated intent ID\n * @property {string} [pending_id] - Associated pending ID\n * @property {Array<string|{slug?: string, name?: string}>} [registered_projects] - Projects for no-match variant\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} PendingCardData\n * @property {string} utterance - The utterance being processed\n * @property {number} createdAt - Creation timestamp (ms epoch)\n * @property {string} [pendingId] - Stable ID for this placeholder\n * @property {number} [completed] - Number of completed sources (for progress)\n * @property {number} [total] - Total number of sources (for progress)\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} TopicCardData\n * @property {Object} topic - The topic object\n * @property {string} topic.id - Topic ID\n * @property {string} topic.label - Topic label\n * @property {string} [topic.type] - Topic type (project, research, personal, exception, compound)\n * @property {Object} staleness - Staleness info\n * @property {number} staleness.seconds - Seconds since last update\n * @property {Object} [latest_result] - Optional latest result\n * @property {string} latest_result.summary - Result summary\n * @property {string} [latest_result.urgency] - Result urgency (critical, high, normal, low)\n * @property {*} [latest_result.data] - Result data\n * @property {boolean} [latest_result.card_fallback] - True when no component matched (fallback card rendered)\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {Object} FallbackCardData\n * @property {string} [summary] - Result summary\n * @property {*} [data] - Result data\n * @property {string} [urgency] - Result urgency\n */\n\n/**\n * @typedef {StuckCardData | FailedCardData | ErrorCardData | PendingCardData | TopicCardData | FallbackCardData} CardData\n * Union type for all card data structures\n */\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (5) Stuck/Failed cards — circuit breaker and terminal failure cards\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build a stuck card for a fenced bead.\n *\n * Plan §10 The Async Path: displayed when a bead is fenced due to repeated\n * refusals or timeout. Shows the bead reference, refusal count, and latest reason.\n *\n * @param {StuckCardData} data - Stuck card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .stuck-card element (data-builtin=\"stuck\")\n */\nfunction createStuckCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card stuck-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'stuck';\n    if (data.bead_id) card.dataset.beadId = String(data.bead_id);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🚧']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Task stuck — needs your input'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (data.message) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'stuck-message', [data.message]));\n    }\n\n    if (data.stuck_reason) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'stuck-reason-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'stuck-reason-label', ['Reason: ']),\n            el('span', 'stuck-reason', [data.stuck_reason])\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    const meta = el('div', 'stuck-meta');\n    let metaChildCount = 0;\n    if (data.refusal_count != null) {\n        meta.appendChild(el('span', 'stuck-refusal-count', [\n            'Refusals: ' + String(data.refusal_count)\n        ]));\n        metaChildCount++;\n    }\n    if (data.bead_id) {\n        if (metaChildCount > 0) {\n            meta.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' • '));\n        }\n        meta.appendChild(el('span', 'stuck-bead-id', ['Bead: ' + data.bead_id]));\n        metaChildCount++;\n    }\n    if (metaChildCount > 0) {\n        card.appendChild(meta);\n    }\n\n    if (data.action_hint) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'stuck-action-hint', [data.action_hint]));\n    }\n\n    // View bead button\n    const viewBtn = el('button', 'stuck-view-bead', ['View bead']);\n    card.appendChild(viewBtn);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n/**\n * Build a failed card for a terminal failure.\n *\n * Plan §10 The Async Path: displayed when an intent fails non-recoverably\n * (worker crash, invalid input, etc.). Shows failure reason and context.\n *\n * @param {FailedCardData} data - Failed card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .failed-card element (data-builtin=\"failed\")\n */\nfunction createFailedCard(data) {\n    data = data || {};\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card failed-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'failed';\n    if (data.bead_id) card.dataset.beadId = String(data.bead_id);\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['❌']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Task failed'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (data.message) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'failed-message', [data.message]));\n    }\n\n    if (data.failure_reason) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'failed-reason-wrap', [\n            el('span', 'failed-reason-label', ['Reason: ']),\n            el('span', 'failed-reason', [data.failure_reason])\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    if (data.error_type) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'failed-error-type', [\n            'Error type: ' + data.error_type\n        ]));\n    }\n\n    if (data.bead_id) {\n        card.appendChild(el('div', 'failed-bead-id', ['Bead: ' + data.bead_id]));\n    }\n\n    // Retry button\n    const retryBtn = el('button', 'failed-retry', ['Retry']);\n    card.appendChild(retryBtn);\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// (1) Generic fallback card — key/value grid over result.data + summary\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Build the generic fallback card for a result that matched no component\n * (plan, Component Library → \"Built-in generic fallback card\"). Key/value grid\n * over result.data plus the summary line — every value escaped per the\n * rendering escaping contract.\n *\n * @param {FallbackCardData} result - Fallback card data structure\n * @returns {HTMLElement} a .fallback-card element (data-builtin=\"fallback\")\n */\nfunction createFallbackCard(result) {\n    result = result || {};\n    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card fallback-card');\n    card.dataset.builtin = 'fallback';\n\n    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['🗒️']),\n        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Result'])\n    ]));\n\n    if (result.summary) {\n        card.appendChild(el('p', 'fallback-summary', [result.summary]));\n    }\n\n    const data = result.data;\n    const rows = _fallbackRows(data);\n    if (rows.length) {\n        const grid = el('div', 'fallback-grid');\n        rows.forEach(function (r) {\n            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-key', [r[0]]));\n            grid.appendChild(el('div', 'fallback-val', [r[1]]));\n        });\n        card.appendChild(grid);\n    }\n\n    if (result.urgency) {\n        card.appendChild(el('span', 'urgency-badge ' + result.urgency, [result.urgency]));\n    }\n\n    return card;\n}\n\n// Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs for the fallback grid.\nfunction _fallbackRows(data) {\n    if (data == null) return [];\n    let obj = data;\n    if (typeof data === 'string') {\n        try { obj = JSON.parse(data); } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n    }\n    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n    }\n    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n    }\n    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n}\n\nfunction _stringify(v) {\n    if (v == null) return '';\n    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n}\n\n// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n// function declarations). formatElapsed,\n        createWelcomeCard: createWelcomeCard,\n        buildContainerChildren: buildContainerChildren,\n        createPendingPlaceholderCard: createPendingPlaceholderCard,\n        createPendingThreadCard: createPendingThreadCard,\n        splitPlaceholderToThreads: splitPlaceholderToThreads,\n        tickPendingElapsed: tickPendingElapsed,\n        applyAgedTreatment: applyAgedTreatment,\n        setPendingProgress: _setProgress,\n        createErrorCard: createErrorCard,\n        createFallbackCard: createFallbackCard,\n        createStuckCard: createStuckCard,\n        createFailedCard: createFailedCard,\n        normalizeErrorVariant: _normalizeVariant,\n        deriveExamples: _deriveExamples,\n        PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS: PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS,\n        _setProgress: _setProgress,  // Also export internal helper\n        _normalizeVariant: _normalizeVariant,  // Also export for testing\n        // Type definitions (for documentation and IDE autocomplete)\n        types: {\n            StuckCardData: 'StuckCardData',\n            FailedCardData: 'FailedCardData',\n            ErrorCardData: 'ErrorCardData',\n            PendingCardData: 'PendingCardData',\n            TopicCardData: 'TopicCardData',\n            FallbackCardData: 'FallbackCardData',\n            CardData: 'CardData'  // Union type\n        }\n    };\n}\n\n// Also expose to inline script in browser (buildContainerChildren is used by loadTopics)\nif (typeof window !== 'undefined') {\n    window.buildContainerChildren = buildContainerChildren;\n    window._setProgress = _setProgress;\n    window.el = el;  // DOM helper for pending cards\n    window.createErrorCard = createErrorCard;  // Error card renderer for SSE error events\n When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above\n9\tthreshold) the result is *flagged* (``card_fallback``) and the client renders\n10\tthe built-in generic fallback card — a novel shape never blanks the canvas.\n11\t\n12\tScope of writes (plan, Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card +\n13\tUI-Regen Agent): the hot-path renderer is the sole writer of ``card_cache`` and\n14\tthe component usage stats (``components.usage_count`` / ``last_used`` and\n15\t``component_usage_patterns``). It NEVER writes component definitions\n16\t(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\n17\tUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n18\t\"\"\"\n19\t\n20\timport html\n21\timport json\n22\timport re\n23\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n24\tfrom logging import getLogger\n25\tfrom typing import Any, Optional\n26\t\n27\tfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n28\t\n29\tlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n30\t\n31\t# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n32\t# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\n33\tDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n34\t\n35\t# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\n36\tDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n37\t\n38\t# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n39\t# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n40\t_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n41\t\n42\t\n43\tdef derive_result_type(\n44\t    intent_type: Optional[str],\n45\t    project_slug: Optional[str],\n46\t    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n47\t) -> str:\n48\t    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n49\t\n50\t    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n51\t      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n52\t    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n53\t      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n54\t      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n55\t    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n56\t\n57\t    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n58\t    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n59\t    \"\"\"\n60\t    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n61\t    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n62\t    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n63\t        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n64\t    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n65\t        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n66\t    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n67\t\n68\t\n69\t@dataclass\n70\tclass RenderOutcome:\n71\t    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n72\t\n73\t    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n74\t    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n75\t    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n76\t    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n77\t    \"\"\"\n78\t\n79\t    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n80\t    component_id: Optional[str]\n81\t    card_fallback: bool\n82\t    layout_bucket: str\n83\t\n84\t\n85\tclass HotPathRenderer:\n86\t    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n87\t\n88\t    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n89\t    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n90\t    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n91\t    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n92\t    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n93\t    \"\"\"\n94\t\n95\t    def __init__(\n96\t        self,\n97\t        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n98\t        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n99\t        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n100\t    ):\n101\t        self.library = library or get_library()\n102\t        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n103\t        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n104\t\n105\t    def render(\n106\t        self,\n107\t        result_id: str,\n108\t        result_type: str,\n109\t        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n110\t        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n111\t        urgency: Optional[str] = None,\n112\t        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n113\t    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n114\t        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n115\t\n116\t        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n117\t        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n118\t        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n119\t        outcome with the rendered fallback HTML and writes nothing to the\n120\t        component DB.\n121\t        \"\"\"\n122\t        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n123\t\n124\t        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n125\t            result_type, self.match_threshold\n126\t        )\n127\t        if component is None:\n128\t            logger.debug(\n129\t                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n130\t                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n131\t                result_type,\n132\t                self.match_threshold,\n133\t                result_id,\n134\t            )\n135\t            # Render the fallback card server-side for SSE streaming\n136\t            fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n137\t                summary=summary,\n138\t                data=result_data,\n139\t                urgency=urgency,\n140\t            )\n141\t            return RenderOutcome(\n142\t                rendered_html=fallback_html,\n143\t                component_id=None,\n144\t                card_fallback=True,\n145\t                layout_bucket=bucket,\n146\t            )\n147\t\n148\t        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n149\t\n150\t        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n151\t        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n152\t        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n153\t        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n154\t        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n155\t        self.library.cache_card(\n156\t            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n157\t        )\n158\t        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n159\t        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n160\t        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n161\t\n162\t        logger.info(\n163\t            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n164\t            result_id,\n165\t            component.id,\n166\t            component.version,\n167\t            result_type,\n168\t        )\n169\t        return RenderOutcome(\n170\t            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n171\t            component_id=component.id,\n172\t            card_fallback=False,\n173\t            layout_bucket=bucket,\n174\t        )\n175\t\n176\t\n177\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n178\t# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n179\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n180\t\n181\tdef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n182\t    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n183\t\n184\t    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n185\t    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n186\t    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n187\t    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n188\t    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n189\t    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n190\t    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n191\t    \"\"\"\n192\t    if not template:\n193\t        return \"\"\n194\t\n195\t    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n196\t        path = match.group(1).strip()\n197\t        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n198\t        if value is None:\n199\t            return \"\"\n200\t        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n201\t\n202\t    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n203\t\n204\t\n205\tdef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n206\t    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n207\t    value: Any = data\n208\t    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n209\t        if isinstance(value, dict):\n210\t            value = value.get(key)\n211\t        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n212\t            value = value[int(key)]\n213\t        else:\n214\t            return None\n215\t    return value\n216\t\n217\t\n218\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n219\t# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n220\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n221\t\n222\tdef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n223\t    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n224\t    if value is None:\n225\t        return ''\n226\t    if isinstance(value, str):\n227\t        return value\n228\t    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n229\t        return str(value)\n230\t    try:\n231\t        return json.dumps(value)\n232\t    except Exception:\n233\t        return str(value)\n234\t\n235\t\n236\tdef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n237\t    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n238\t    if data is None:\n239\t        return []\n240\t\n241\t    obj = data\n242\t\n243\t    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n244\t    if isinstance(data, str):\n245\t        try:\n246\t            obj = json.loads(data)\n247\t        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n248\t            return [('value', data)]\n249\t\n250\t    # Handle array\n251\t    if isinstance(obj, list):\n252\t        rows = []\n253\t        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n254\t            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n255\t        return rows\n256\t\n257\t    # Handle object\n258\t    if isinstance(obj, dict):\n259\t        rows = []\n260\t        for k in list(obj.keys())[:50]:  # Max 50 rows\n261\t            rows.append((k, _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n262\t        return rows\n263\t\n264\t    # Handle primitive\n265\t    return [('value', str(obj))]\n266\t\n267\t\n268\tdef render_fallback_card(\n269\t    summary: str | None = None,\n270\t    data: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n271\t    urgency: str | None = None,\n272\t) -> str:\n273\t    \"\"\"Render the generic fallback card HTML (server-side version of createFallbackCard).\n274\t\n275\t    This generates the same HTML structure as the client-side createFallbackCard()\n276\t    function in canvas.js, ensuring the fallback card can be streamed via SSE\n277\t    and injected directly into the canvas without a blank canvas state.\n278\t\n279\t    All dynamic values are HTML-escaped per the escaping contract.\n280\t\n281\t    Args:\n282\t        summary: Optional result summary\n283\t        data: Optional result data dict for key/value grid\n284\t        urgency: Optional urgency level for badge\n285\t\n286\t    Returns:\n287\t        HTML string for the fallback card\n288\t    \"\"\"\n289\t    try:\n290\t        # Build fallback card HTML\n291\t        card_parts = ['<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\">']\n292\t\n293\t        # Header with icon and title\n294\t        card_parts.append('  <div class=\"builtin-header\">')\n295\t        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-icon\">{html.escape(\"🗒️\", quote=False)}</span>')\n296\t        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-title\">{html.escape(\"Result\", quote=False)}</span>')\n297\t        card_parts.append('  </div>')\n298\t\n299\t        # Summary paragraph\n300\t        if summary:\n301\t            card_parts.append(f'  <p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary, quote=False)}</p>')\n302\t\n303\t        # Key/value grid from data\n304\t        if data:\n305\t            rows = _fallback_rows(data)\n306\t            if rows:\n307\t                card_parts.append('  <div class=\"fallback-grid\">')\n308\t                for key, value in rows:\n309\t                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-key\">{html.escape(key, quote=False)}</div>')\n310\t                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-val\">{html.escape(value, quote=False)}</div>')\n311\t                card_parts.append('  </div>')\n312\t\n313\t        # Urgency badge\n314\t        if urgency:\n315\t            card_parts.append(f'  <span class=\"urgency-badge {html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}\">{html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}</span>')\n316\t\n317\t        card_parts.append('</div>')\n318\t\n319\t        return '\\n'.join(card_parts)\n320\t\n321\t    except Exception as e:\n322\t        # Fallback render failures should not crash the dispatch flow\n323\t        logger.error(f\"Fallback card render failed: {e}\")\n324\t        # Return minimal safe fallback HTML\n325\t        return f'<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\"><p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary or \"Result\", quote=False)}</p></div>'\n326\t\n327\t\n328\tdef select_rendered_card(\n329\t    result_type: str,\n330\t    result_data: dict[str, Any],\n331\t    result_id: str,\n332\t    layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n333\t    match_threshold: Optional[rendered_html].\n337\t\n338\t    Pure SQL lookup — no LLM call in this path. It returns Optional[str] directly, unlike render() which\n346\t    returns a RenderOutcome object with additional metadata.\n347\t\n348\t    Args:\n349\t        result_type: The result type to match component template with result.data\n380\t    rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n381\t\n382\t    # Write to card_cache\n383\t    lib.cache_card(\n384\t component_id=component.id,\n394\t        result_type=result_type,\n395\t        match_score=1.0,\n396\t        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n397\t    )\n398\t\n399\t    logger.info(\n400\t result_type,\n405\t    )\n406\t\n407\t    return rendered_html\n408\t\n409\t\n410\t# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n411\t# Singleton\n412\t# no-LLM server-side card selector (the dispatch hot path).\n\nOn every dispatch the server picks the component to render a result\n*deterministically* — the highest ``match_score`` row in\n``component_usage_patterns`` for the result's ``result_type``, no LLM call —\nfills that component's template with the result data per layout bucket, writes\nthe rendered HTML to ``card_cache``, records the usage, and returns it for SSE\nstreaming. When nothing matches (a first-ever result shape, or no score above\nthreshold) the result is *flagged* (``card_fallback``) and the client renders\nthe built-in generic fallback card — a novel shape never blanks the canvas.\n\nScope of writes (plan, Component Library → Built-in generic fallback card +\nUI-Regen Agent): the hot-path renderer is the sole writer of ``card_cache`` and\nthe component usage stats (``components.usage_count`` / ``last_used`` and\n``component_usage_patterns``). It NEVER writes component definitions\n(``components`` rows, ``component_versions``, ``component_tags``) — that is the\nUI-regen agent's job alone, so the two never race on a component's identity.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport html\nimport json\nimport re\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..components.library import Component, ComponentLibrary, get_library\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# A component_usage_patterns row must clear this to be selected (plan: \"highest\n# match_score ... no LLM\"; below this the result falls to the built-in fallback).\nDEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD = 0.7\n\n# The hot path renders one card per intent thread at a single layout bucket.\nDEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET = \"normal\"\n\n# Flat ``{{field.path}}`` token (no loops/conditionals) — the same substitution\n# contract the UI-regen generator is told to emit (see ui_regen.py).\n_PLACEHOLDER = re.compile(r\"\\{\\{([^}]+)\\}\\}\")\n\n\ndef derive_result_type(\n    intent_type: Optional[str],\n    project_slug: Optional[str],\n    lookup_kind: Optional[str] = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Derive the deterministic card-selector key written to ``results.result_type``.\n\n    - intent-derived: ``\"{intent_type}:{project_slug}\"`` — one per intent thread\n      (the aggregated thread card), never per fetch source.\n    - lookup threads insert the router's ``lookup_kind``:\n      ``\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{project_slug}\"`` (e.g. ``lookup:logs:ibkr-mcp`` vs\n      ``lookup:config:ibkr-mcp`` — distinct keys, distinct cards).\n    - monitoring-originated rows: ``\"monitoring:{project_slug}\"``.\n\n    A missing project slug collapses to ``\"general\"`` so the key is always a\n    non-empty, colon-segmented string the selector can index on.\n    \"\"\"\n    slug = project_slug or \"general\"\n    itype = intent_type or \"status\"\n    if itype == \"monitoring\":\n        return f\"monitoring:{slug}\"\n    if itype == \"lookup\" and lookup_kind:\n        return f\"lookup:{lookup_kind}:{slug}\"\n    return f\"{itype}:{slug}\"\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RenderOutcome:\n    \"\"\"Result of a hot-path render: either a real component render, or a fallback flag.\n\n    ``rendered_html`` is None exactly when no component matched (the client must\n    render the built-in generic fallback card). ``card_fallback`` is the boolean\n    mirror persisted onto the result row so loadTopics()/the SSE consumer both\n    see the same decision without re-running the selector.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    rendered_html: Optional[str]\n    component_id: Optional[str]\n    card_fallback: bool\n    layout_bucket: str\n\n\nclass HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic server-side card selector — never on the LLM path.\n\n    Selection is a single ``component_usage_patterns`` lookup on\n    ``result_type``; on a match it fills the template (escaping every\n    interpolated value — the render-path escaping boundary), writes\n    ``card_cache``, and bumps the usage stats. On no match it returns a\n    fallback outcome and writes nothing to the component DB.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n        match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n        layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    ):\n        self.library = library or get_library()\n        self.match_threshold = match_threshold\n        self.layout_bucket = layout_bucket\n\n    def render(\n        self,\n        result_id: str,\n        result_type: str,\n        result_data: dict[str, Any],\n        summary: Optional[str] = None,\n        urgency: Optional[str] = None,\n        layout_bucket: Optional[str] = None,\n    ) -> RenderOutcome:\n        \"\"\"Select + render a card for one result. Deterministic, no LLM.\n\n        On a match: fills the template (escaped), writes card_cache keyed\n        ``(result_id, component_id, layout_bucket)``, and records the usage\n        pattern + usage stats. On no match / below threshold: returns a fallback\n        outcome with the rendered fallback HTML and writes nothing to the\n        component DB.\n        \"\"\"\n        bucket = layout_bucket or self.layout_bucket\n\n        component = self.library.select_component_for_result_type(\n            result_type, self.match_threshold\n        )\n        if component is None:\n            logger.debug(\n                \"hot-path fallback: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n                \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n                result_type,\n                self.match_threshold,\n                result_id,\n            )\n            # Render the fallback card server-side for SSE streaming\n            fallback_html = render_fallback_card(\n                summary=summary,\n                data=result_data,\n                urgency=urgency,\n            )\n            return RenderOutcome(\n                rendered_html=fallback_html,\n                component_id=None,\n                card_fallback=True,\n                layout_bucket=bucket,\n            )\n\n        rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n        # The server is the sole writer of card_cache + usage stats (write-scope\n        # separation). cache_card bumps components.usage_count/last_used;\n        # record_usage_pattern updates component_usage_patterns (sample_count,\n        # last_matched, running match_score). Neither touches component\n        # definitions — that is the UI-regen agent's job.\n        self.library.cache_card(\n            result_id, component.id, component.version, bucket, rendered_html\n        )\n        # A hot-path match is a confirmed, high-confidence usage — record at 1.0\n        # so reliably-matching components trend toward the top of the selector.\n        self.library.record_usage_pattern(component.id, result_type, match_score=1.0)\n\n        logger.info(\n            \"hot-path rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n            result_id,\n            component.id,\n            component.version,\n            result_type,\n        )\n        return RenderOutcome(\n            rendered_html=rendered_html,\n            component_id=component.id,\n            card_fallback=False,\n            layout_bucket=bucket,\n        )\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Template fill — the render-path escaping boundary\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef fill_template(template: str, result_data: dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Flat dot-path substitution with HTML escaping at fill time.\n\n    Every ``{{field.path}}`` token is replaced with the HTML-escaped value at\n    that dot-path (list indices via numeric segments, e.g. ``{{pods.0.name}}``).\n    Unknown paths resolve to \"\". This is the escaping boundary the plan binds\n    every server-filled card to (UI-Regen Agent → \"Escaping contract\"): a\n    markup-looking log line interpolated here renders as literal text instead of\n    breaking layout or executing in the canvas. No loops or conditionals — the\n    generator is instructed to emit only flat placeholders.\n    \"\"\"\n    if not template:\n        return \"\"\n\n    def replace_value(match: re.Match) -> str:\n        path = match.group(1).strip()\n        value = _get_nested_value(result_data, path)\n        if value is None:\n            return \"\"\n        return html.escape(str(value), quote=False)\n\n    return _PLACEHOLDER.sub(replace_value, template)\n\n\ndef _get_nested_value(data: dict[str, Any], path: str) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"Resolve a dot-path against a dict/list (numeric segments index lists).\"\"\"\n    value: Any = data\n    for key in path.split(\".\"):\n        if isinstance(value, dict):\n            value = value.get(key)\n        elif isinstance(value, list) and key.isdigit() and int(key) < len(value):\n            value = value[int(key)]\n        else:\n            return None\n    return value\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Generic fallback card renderer (server-side)\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef _stringify_value(value: Any) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Stringify a value for fallback card display (mirrors client-side _stringify).\"\"\"\n    if value is None:\n        return ''\n    if isinstance(value, str):\n        return value\n    if isinstance(value, (int, float, bool)):\n        return str(value)\n    try:\n        return json.dumps(value)\n    except Exception:\n        return str(value)\n\n\ndef _fallback_rows(data: Any) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:\n    \"\"\"Flatten result.data into [key, value] string pairs (mirrors client-side _fallbackRows).\"\"\"\n    if data is None:\n        return []\n\n    obj = data\n\n    # Handle string - try to parse as JSON\n    if isinstance(data, str):\n        try:\n            obj = json.loads(data)\n        except json.JSONDecodeError:\n            return [('value', data)]\n\n    # Handle array\n    if isinstance(obj, list):\n        rows = []\n        for i, v in enumerate(obj[:50]):  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((str(i), _stringify_value(v)))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle object\n    if isinstance(obj, dict):\n        rows = []\n        for k in list(obj.keys())[:50]:  # Max 50 rows\n            rows.append((k, _stringify_value(obj[k])))\n        return rows\n\n    # Handle primitive\n    return [('value', str(obj))]\n\n\ndef render_fallback_card(\n    summary: str | None = None,\n    data: dict[str, Any] | None = None,\n    urgency: str | None = None,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Render the generic fallback card HTML (server-side version of createFallbackCard).\n\n    This generates the same HTML structure as the client-side createFallbackCard()\n    function in canvas.js, ensuring the fallback card can be streamed via SSE\n    and injected directly into the canvas without a blank canvas state.\n\n    All dynamic values are HTML-escaped per the escaping contract.\n\n    Args:\n        summary: Optional result summary\n        data: Optional result data dict for key/value grid\n        urgency: Optional urgency level for badge\n\n    Returns:\n        HTML string for the fallback card\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Build fallback card HTML\n        card_parts = ['<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\">']\n\n        # Header with icon and title\n        card_parts.append('  <div class=\"builtin-header\">')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-icon\">{html.escape(\"🗒️\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append(f'    <span class=\"builtin-title\">{html.escape(\"Result\", quote=False)}</span>')\n        card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Summary paragraph\n        if summary:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary, quote=False)}</p>')\n\n        # Key/value grid from data\n        if data:\n            rows = _fallback_rows(data)\n            if rows:\n                card_parts.append('  <div class=\"fallback-grid\">')\n                for key, value in rows:\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-key\">{html.escape(key, quote=False)}</div>')\n                    card_parts.append(f'    <div class=\"fallback-val\">{html.escape(value, quote=False)}</div>')\n                card_parts.append('  </div>')\n\n        # Urgency badge\n        if urgency:\n            card_parts.append(f'  <span class=\"urgency-badge {html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}\">{html.escape(urgency, quote=False)}</span>')\n\n        card_parts.append('</div>')\n\n        return '\\n'.join(card_parts)\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        # Fallback render failures should not crash the dispatch flow\n        logger.error(f\"Fallback card render failed: {e}\")\n        # Return minimal safe fallback HTML\n        return f'<div class=\"builtin-card fallback-card\" data-builtin=\"fallback\"><p class=\"fallback-summary\">{html.escape(summary or \"Result\", quote=False)}</p></div>'\n\n\ndef select_rendered_card(\n    result_type: str,\n    result_data: dict[str, Any],\n    result_id: str,\n    layout_bucket: str = DEFAULT_LAYOUT_BUCKET,\n    match_threshold: float = DEFAULT_MATCH_THRESHOLD,\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n) -> Optional[str]:\n    \"\"\"Deterministic component selector: (result_type, result.data) → Optional[rendered_html].\n\n    Pure SQL lookup — no LLM call in this path. Queries component_usage_patterns\n    for the highest match_score where result_type matches. If found and score above\n    threshold: fills component template with result.data, writes to card_cache, and\n    returns rendered HTML. If no match or below threshold: returns None (triggers\n    fallback rendering by the caller).\n\n    This is the server-side deterministic component selector specified in the task\n    acceptance criteria. It returns Optional[str] directly, unlike render() which\n    returns a RenderOutcome object with additional metadata.\n\n    Args:\n        result_type: The result type to match against component_usage_patterns\n        result_data: The result data dict to fill into the component template\n        result_id: The result ID for card_cache write\n        layout_bucket: The layout bucket to use (default: 'normal')\n        match_threshold: Minimum match score threshold (default: 0.7)\n        library: Optional ComponentLibrary instance (uses singleton if not provided)\n\n    Returns:\n        Rendered HTML string if a component matched, None if no match or below threshold\n    \"\"\"\n    lib = library or get_library()\n\n    # Query component_usage_patterns for highest match_score where result_type matches\n    component = lib.select_component_for_result_type(\n        result_type=result_type,\n        match_threshold=match_threshold,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n    )\n\n    # If no component matched or below threshold, return None (triggers fallback)\n    if component is None:\n        logger.debug(\n            \"select_rendered_card: no component matched result_type=%s \"\n            \"(threshold %.2f) for result %s\",\n            result_type,\n            match_threshold,\n            result_id,\n        )\n        return None\n\n    # On match: fill component template with result.data\n    rendered_html = fill_template(component.html_template, result_data)\n\n    # Write to card_cache\n    lib.cache_card(\n        result_id=result_id,\n        component_id=component.id,\n        component_version=component.version,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n        rendered_html=rendered_html,\n    )\n\n    # Record usage pattern (hot-path match is high confidence, so score=1.0)\n    lib.record_usage_pattern(\n        component_id=component.id,\n        result_type=result_type,\n        match_score=1.0,\n        layout_bucket=layout_bucket,\n    )\n\n    logger.info(\n        \"select_rendered_card: rendered result %s with component %s v%s (result_type=%s)\",\n        result_id,\n        component.id,\n        component.version,\n        result_type,\n    )\n\n    return rendered_html\n\n\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# Singleton\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n_renderer: Optional[HotPathRenderer] = None\n\n\ndef get_renderer(\n    library: Optional[ComponentLibrary] = None,\n    reset: bool = False,\n) -> HotPathRenderer:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the process-wide hot-path renderer singleton.\n\n    Pass ``library=`` (and ``reset=True``) to rebind it — how tests point the\n    renderer at an isolated component DB without touching the production\n    ``data/components.db`` singleton.\n    \"\"\"\n    global _renderer\n    if _renderer is None or reset or library is not None:\n        _renderer = HotPathRenderer(library=library)\n    return _renderer\n","numLines":431,"startLine":1,"totalLines":431}}}

tests for component and fallback card rendering via SSE (bead adc-35eq).\n\nThese tests verify that both render paths work correctly through the full\ndispatch → SSE → render pipeline:\n\n1. get_broadcaster()\n    if not getattr(b, \"_running\", False):\n        await b.start()\n    return b\n\n\nasync def _collect_sse_events(broadcaster, conn, wanted_types, *, timeout=2.0):\n    \"\"\"Collect SSE events until all wanted types arrive (excluding 'connected').\"\"\"\n    wanted = set(wanted_types)\n    collected = []\n\n    async def drain():\n        async for wire in broadcaster.event_generator(conn):\n            for etype, data in parse_sse_stream(wire):\n                if etype == \"connected\":\n                    continue\n                collected.append((etype, data))\n                if wanted <= {e for e, _ in collected}:\n                    return\n\n    task = asyncio.create_task(drain())\n    try:\n        await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n    except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n        task.cancel()\n        raise\n    return collected\n\n\n# --- Component path tests (seeded match)       )\n\n            # Collect SSE events\n            events = await _collect_sse_events(b, conn, [\"result_created\"])\n            assert len(events) >= 1\n\n            etype, data = events[0]\n tests for dispatch → hot-path selector → SSE → canvas rendering flow.\n\nThese tests verify the complete flow from HTTP request through intent router,\nhot-path component selector, SSE broadcast, to canvas rendering. They ensure:\n\n1. Dispatch with known matching component → cached HTML renders via SSE\n2. Dispatch with novel shape → fallback card renders via SSE\n3. NO blank canvas ever (continuity invariant)\n\nTests use the real /dispatch endpoint, SSE broadcaster, and canvas.js renderer\nwith no browser or network dependencies (headless Node.js DOM runner).\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom unittest.mock import Mock\n\nimport pytest\n\nimport src.main as main_mod\nimport src.session.store as store_mod\nfrom src.components.library import ComponentLibrary, get_library\nfrom src.render.hot_path import HotPathRenderer, get_renderer\nfrom src.sse.broadcaster import SSEEvent, get_broadcaster\nfrom tests.e2e.canvas_render import (\n    node_available,\n    parse_sse_stream,\n    render_cards,\n)\n\npytestmark = pytest.mark.skipif(\n    not node_available(), reason=\"node not on PATH — cannot drive canvas DOM runner\"\n)\n\n\n# --- fixtures ----------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\nasync def isolated_store(tmp_path, monkeypatch):\n    \"\"\"Isolated session store for each test (never touches data/session.db).\"\"\"\n    tmp_db = tmp_path / \"test-dispatch-e2e.db\"\n    tmp_components = tmp_path / \"test-components.db\"\n    monkeypatch.setenv(\"ADC_DB_PATH\", str(tmp_db))\n    monkeypatch.setenv(\"ADC_COMPONENTS_DB\", str(tmp_components))\n\n    from src.components.library import _library_instance as saved_lib_instance\n    from src.render.hot_path import _renderer as saved_renderer\n\n    saved_store = store_mod._store\n    saved_main_store = main_mod._store\n    saved_main_component_library = main_mod._component_library\n\n    store_mod._store = None\n    main_mod._store = None\n    main_mod._component_library = None\n\n    # Use the isolated library from the fixture, not the global singleton\n        lib = main_mod._component_library\n        assert lib is not None, \"Component library should be initialized by fixture\"\n\n        # High confidence match\n            layout_bucket=\"normal\",\n        )\n\n        # Broadcast result_created event (simulating what /dispatch does)\n        b = await _started_broadcaster()\n        conn = b.register(\n            surface_id=surface_id, session_id=session_id, surface_type=\"canvas\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # pods value filled in\n            assert \"running\" in render_outcome.rendered_html  # status value filled in\n\n            # Broadcast the event with component rendered HTML\n            await b.broadcast(\n                SSEEvent(\n                    event_type=\"result_created\",\n                    data={\n                        \"intent_id\": \"intent-1\",\n                        \"topic_id\": \"topic-1\",\n                        \"summary\": \"3 pods running\",\n                        \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n                        \"component_id\": component.id,\n                        \"card_fallback\": False,\n                    },\n                    rendered_html=render_outcome.rendered_html,\n                    target_surface_id=surface_id,\n                )\n            )\n\n            #
